We make it simple for DMEs to expand into a growing market with a product families already trust. The Cubby Bed is easy to set up, supported by clear documentation, and backed by a dedicated care team that guides families through funding. Partnering with us helps you diversify your portfolio, access thousands of qualified leads, and benefit from our ongoing resources. This page outlines how we work with DMEs, the tools available, and the steps to get started. You can also download our Cubby Beds brochure to share with your team or referral sources. Do you bill insurance? Do you serve pediatrics? Do you support families with Medicaid or private insurance? Do you work with clinicians or referral sources? If yes, you’re a great candidate to distribute Cubby Beds!

chunks.sectionTitle: Medicaid requirements for Cubby Bed coverage
chunks.text: Your role is critical in obtaining insurance coverage for a Cubby Bed. We want to make this process easier. This page explains the general steps for insurance approval in your state and who can help along the way. Understanding these requirements will ensure smoother communication, timely submissions, and increase the likelihood of coverage.
All other states
Last updated: 2/2026 • External links may change; we review this information biannually.

For caregivers Schedule an appointment with your loved one’s doctor. Before your visit, print both the Alabama Medicaid Referral Form and the Prescription Form in the Required documents packet. If you do not have access to a printer, ask the provider to print them for you at the appointment. Discuss the medical need for a Cubby Bed during your visit and ask the doctor to fill out the printed forms. Obtain a letter of medical necessity (LMN). It is highly recommended that it be written by an Occupational Therapist (OT) or Physical Therapist (PT). If you do not have access to an OT or PT, consult with your chosen medical supplier (see next step), or if necessary, a physician can write the LMN. Use our Safety Needs and Concerns Worksheet to write down your loved one’s safety concerns and any other solutions you’ve tried that didn’t work. The level of detail you provide is crucial to ensure your medical documents fully capture your loved one's need for a Cubby Bed. Locate a medical supplier that carries Cubby Beds and accepts your insurance. Follow up with the medical supplier: Confirm they’ve received all the necessary documents and ensure they’ve submitted your request to Medicaid. For more information on how to get a Cubby Bed, visit our website.

For doctors Complete the Prescription Form, which is included in the Required documents packet. Fill out your portion of the Alabama Medicaid Referral Form. Once your patient has partnered with a medical supplier, please send these documents directly to that supplier. You may need to work with the supplier to provide revisions or additional paperwork that insurance requests for the authorization process or for appealing a denial. Medical necessity and safety concerns should be clearly documented in the patient’s chart.

For OT/PT Work with the family to write a letter of medical necessity (LMN). Use the guidance provided in the Required documents packet for the details that need to be included. Ask the family to share their completed Safety Needs and Concerns Worksheet with you, which will help you write a detailed letter. Alabama requires the letter of medical necessity (LMN) to also include a monitoring plan with the following details:→ The patient will be checked/monitored every 3-4 hours while in bed→ The approximate time frame the bed will be used and how the user will be monitored→ Who lives with the patient→ Document any specific instances of harm or likelihood of harm Once your patient has partnered with a medical supplier, please send the LMN directly to that supplier. You may need to work with the supplier to provide revisions or additional paperwork that insurance requests for the authorization process or for appealing a denial.

For medical supplier The medical supplier must receive the prescription, Alabama Medicaid Referral form, and the letter of medical necessity (LMN) within 90 days of the date they are signed. Communicate regularly with the family and providers to improve the chance of approval.
Sometimes Medicaid requests are denied, which is more common than you might expect. If that happens, our Medicaid Appeal Process Guide explains the steps you can take.

For OT/PT Work with the family to write a letter of medical necessity (LMN). Use the guidance provided in the Required documents packet for the details that need to be included.→ To increase the chance of coverage, please address the language in Connecticut’s definition of medical necessity. The Connecticut DSS definition of medical necessity is “...health care provided to correct or diminish the adverse effects of a medical condition or mental illness; to assist an individual in attaining or maintaining an optimal level of health; to diagnose a condition; or to prevent a medical condition from occurring.” Once your patient has partnered with a medical supplier, please send the LMN directly to that supplier. You may need to work with the supplier to provide revisions or additional paperwork that insurance requests for the authorization process or for appealing a denial.

For medical supplier Connecticut Medicaid policy guidelines state that safety beds are non-covered due to being considered investigational. However, the policy clearly states that if these guidelines conflict with the definition of Medical Necessity, the definition of Medical Necessity from DSS shall prevail. (Please see above under OT/PT for the official DSS definition.) Communicate regularly with the family and providers to improve the chance of approval.

chunks.text: Your role is critical in obtaining insurance coverage for a Cubby Bed. We want to make this process easier. This page explains the general steps for insurance approval in your state and who can help along the way. Understanding these requirements will ensure smoother communication, timely submissions, and increase the likelihood of coverage.
All other states
Last updated: 2/2026 • External links may change; we review this information biannually.

For caregivers Your first step is to locate a medical supplier that carries Cubby Beds and accepts your insurance. The supplier will help guide you through the insurance process and assist in gathering the required documents. Schedule an appointment with your loved one’s doctor. Before your visit, print the Prescription Form in the Required documents packet. If you do not have access to a printer, ask the provider to print it for you at the appointment. Discuss the medical need for a Cubby Bed during your visit and ask the doctor to fill out the printed Prescription Form. Locate a medical supplier that carries Cubby Beds and accepts your insurance. The supplier will help guide you through the insurance process and assist in gathering the required documents. They will also need to be present during an evaluation performed by an OT or PT in order to receive a letter of medical necessity (outlined in the next step). Obtain a letter of medical necessity (LMN) from an Occupational Therapist (OT) or Physical Therapist (PT). An LMN written by a physician will NOT be accepted. If you do not have access to an OT or PT, consult with your chosen medical supplier. Use our Safety Needs and Concerns Worksheet to write down your loved one’s safety concerns and any other solutions you’ve tried that didn’t work. The level of detail you provide is crucial to ensure your medical documents fully capture your loved one's need for a Cubby Bed. The medical supplier will need to be present at the evaluation by the OT or PT, so make sure you are connected with a supplier before requesting the LMN. The details discussed on the Safety Needs and Concerns Worksheet should be documented in your loved one's medical record. If the information is not in their record, request that the doctor add it to the chart notes. Follow up with the medical supplier: Confirm they’ve received all the necessary documents and ensure they’ve submitted your request to Medicaid. For more information on how to get a Cubby Bed, visit our website.

For doctors Complete the Prescription Form, which is included in the Required documents packet. Medical necessity and safety concerns should be clearly documented in the patient’s chart. Explain the significance of a letter of medical necessity (LMN) to the patient, so they have a plan to obtain one. Georgia Medicaid requires the letter to be written by an OT or PT, and will NOT be accepted by a physician. Once your patient has partnered with a medical supplier, please send the Prescription Form directly to the supplier. You may need to work with the supplier to provide revisions or additional paperwork that insurance requests for the authorization process or for appealing a denial.

For OT/PT Work with the family to write a letter of medical necessity (LMN). Use the guidance provided in the Required documents packet for the details that need to be included. Ask the family to share their completed Safety Needs and Concerns Worksheet with you, which will help you write a detailed letter. In addition to the recommended guidance in the above packet, be sure to address the following:→ Patients with severe behavioral disorders need to have a documented plan for behavioral management.→ Where is the member currently sleeping and why is it no longer working?→ What are the specific features of the requested safe bed that are medically necessary for this member?→ Provide your written plan for monitoring the member that has been approved by the ordering physician describing when the bed will be used, how the member will be monitored, how all the member’s needs will be met while enclosed in the bed (toileting, eating, hydration, skin care and general safety), and an explanation of how any documented medical conditions (e.g., seizures) will be managed while the bed is in use.→ Must provide safety, cognitive, behavioral and boundary concerns. Evaluation should specify the need for a safe bed, including how the requested bed was chosen over other options.→ Any requests for a built-in technology hub, or any other specific features or components, should include specific documentation on the need for the features. Once your patient has partnered with a medical supplier, please send the LMN directly to that supplier. You may need to work with the supplier to provide revisions or additional paperwork that insurance requests for the authorization process or for appealing a denial.

For medical supplier Georgia requires the Certification of Medical Necessity for Custom Durable Medical Equipment form signed by the ordering physician, in addition to the OT/PT. An ATP will need to be present during the evaluation by the OT or PT. Communicate regularly with the family and providers to improve the chance of approval.
Sometimes Medicaid requests are denied, which is more common than you might expect. If that happens, our Medicaid Appeal Process Guide explains the steps you can take.

Documentation should address if the patient would be at risk for injury, harm, or putting themselves or others in danger without the bed. Discuss the medical need for a Cubby Bed during your visit and ask the doctor to fill out the printed Prescription Form. Obtain a letter of medical necessity (LMN). It is highly recommended that it be written by an Occupational Therapist (OT) or Physical Therapist (PT). If you do not have access to an OT or PT, consult with your chosen medical supplier (see next step), or if necessary, a physician can write the LMN. Use our Safety Needs and Concerns Worksheet to write down your loved one’s safety concerns and any other solutions you’ve tried that didn’t work. The level of detail you provide is crucial to ensure your medical documents fully capture your loved one's need for a Cubby Bed. The details discussed on the Safety Needs and Concerns Worksheet should be documented in your loved one's medical record. If the information is not in their record, request that the doctor add it to the chart notes. Locate a medical supplier that carries Cubby Beds and accepts your insurance. Follow up with the medical supplier: Confirm they’ve received all the necessary documents and ensure they’ve submitted your request to Medicaid. For more information on how to get a Cubby Bed, visit our website.

Medical necessity and safety concerns should be clearly documented in the patient’s chart.

For OT/PT Work with the family to write a letter of medical necessity (LMN). Use the guidance provided in the Required documents packet for the details that need to be included. Ask the family to share their completed Safety Needs and Concerns Worksheet with you, which will help you write a detailed letter.→ Wisconsin Medicaid will not approve coverage for restraint or behavioral management unless there is an imminent risk of harm to the individual. → Medical necessity must be clearly and thoroughly documented. Medicaid will only provide coverage in cases where there is a history of daily seizure activity or self-injurious behaviors, such as head banging, that have resulted in documented physical injury. Once your patient has partnered with a medical supplier, please send the LMN directly to that supplier. This article is a starting point for medical suppliers (DME partners) working through how to code and bill a Cubby Bed. Coding and reimbursement vary by state and by payer, so use this as orientation, then confirm the specifics with your payer and your own contracts.

HCPCS Code Description E1399 Durable medical equipment, miscellaneous E0328 Hospital bed, pediatric, manual, 360 degree side enclosures, top of headboard, footboard and side rails up to 24 inches above the spring, includes mattress E0329 Hospital bed, pediatric, electric or semi-electric, 360 degree side enclosures, top of headboard, footboard and side rails up to 24 inches above the spring, includes mattress E0316 Safety enclosure frame/canopy for use with hospital bed, any type

chunks.sectionTitle: How to decide which code to use in your state
chunks.text: There's no single right answer here, because coding direction comes from your payer and your state. A few principles can help you land in the right place. When there isn't a product-specific HCPCS code that accurately matches an item's specifications and medical application, a miscellaneous DME code is generally the option that gets considered. For the Cubby Bed, a miscellaneous code is usually the most accurate fit, and it's the one with which our DME partners tend to see the most consistent success. It's worth understanding why the pediatric enclosed-bed codes (E0328 and E0329) usually don't apply. The Cubby Bed isn't verified by the PDAC (Pricing, Data Analysis, and Coding contractor) under a product-specific HCPCS, so from a federal coding-assignment standpoint, it wouldn't be billed under E0328 or E0329. Those descriptors also define a bed with either electric or manual head & foot articulation, which does not match Cubby Beds. That said, some states and payers direct a specific code as their pathway to coverage. If a payer names a code in writing, or its coverage criteria call for one, follow that directive and review it alongside the plan's coverage criteria. Always look for payer-specific guidance first.

chunks.sectionTitle: What payers need when you bill a miscellaneous code
chunks.text: A miscellaneous code doesn't map to a specific device, so payers can't match it to a product on their own. Your submission has to connect those dots for them. In practice, that means showing: What item is being billed, validated with the make and model, order forms, and quotes Explanation of use and components Why there isn't a more specific code for the item Confirmation of medical necessity for the item Keep in mind, there aren't many product-specific HCPCS codes for non-wheelchair DME, so this supporting documentation does a lot of the work in a miscellaneous-code submission.

Reimbursement depends on the payer and, for miscellaneous codes, on your contract. Medicaid fee-for-service (FFS) has a publicly available reimbursement calculation you can reference. Other payers, including MCOs, typically use an industry-recognized pricing methodology: Methodology How it works MSRP minus a discount % Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price, reduced by a set percentage Usual + customary minus a discount % Your provider billed charges, reduced by a set percentage Cost plus a markup % Your product cost increased by a set percentage These pricing methodologies aren't set by Cubby Beds. They're determined by your individual contract with each payer when you go contracted, or in-network, with them. That contract includes a fee schedule or a pricing addendum, and the reimbursement for a miscellaneous code lives there. If you're running into reimbursement challenges, start with your contract to find the rate tied to that code, then reach out to the payer's provider contracting team if you'd like to negotiate it. As the manufacturer, Cubby Beds doesn't determine these rates, they sit between you and the payer.

Look for payer-specific guidance on which HCPCS to use before you bill. Some payers will tell you in writing which code to use. That written directive is your strongest starting point, so pair it with the plan's coverage criteria. To request a quote, please email: orders@cubbybeds.com. Please note: quotes do not include specific billing codes. Disclaimer: This article reflects the opinions and suggestions of Cubby Beds and is for informational purposes only. It isn't legal, coding, or billing advice. Please consult your own legal counsel or compliance officer for guidance on any legal or regulatory matters. Cubby Beds assumes no responsibility or liability for any action taken or not taken based on this content. For more extensive resources, please visit our Supplier Portal.

chunks.sectionTitle: Safety Features: Cubby Bed & Technology Hubs

Below are the essential safety features for both the bed frame and the specific hub models. Note: The Cubby 2 Bed with Dream Hub began shipping out in Spring 2026. Orders fulfilled before this date included the Cubby 1 model. Additionally, you can easily identify your version by looking for an embroidered 'Cubby' logo on the front of the canopy (indicates Cubby 2) and/or a rainbow-shaped light on the hub (indicates the Dream Hub). For additional details about each model, click here.

chunks.pageTitle: Safety Features: Cubby Bed & Technology Hubs
chunks.text: Safety & Monitoring by Model

chunks.pageTitle: Safety Features: Cubby Bed & Technology Hubs
chunks.text: Safety Sheet System: A critical feature that mitigates the risk of entrapment and burrowing. Our design addresses the FDA's 7 Zones of Entrapment guidelines. Each Cubby comes with two (2) breathable microfiber sheets that zip and lock directly into the side walls of the canopy, ensuring your child cannot get underneath the mattress. Tension Padding: We avoid toxic or environmentally harmful foams. Instead, the Cubby uses "Tension Padding"—a unique design where specialized fabric is tautly stretched across our custom frame to create a soft, drum-like "cocoon" that safely absorbs impact. Secured Zipper Pockets: Designed for "little Houdinis," every entry point features a specialized zipper pocket. This allows you to tuck the zipper pull out of reach, preventing elopement and ensuring your child remains safely inside the bed.

chunks.pageTitle: Safety Features: Cubby Bed & Technology Hubs
chunks.text: Shared Safety Features (Both Hubs): 2-Way Audio: Remotely speak to your child via the smartphone app (both models) or the handheld monitor (Dream Hub). Environmental Sensors: Monitor room temperature and humidity to ensure a safe sleep environment. Motion & Sound Alerts: Receive notifications if your child is active or if the camera detects a sudden noise. Technology Hub (Wyze) App-Based Monitoring: All video, audio, and sensor data are accessed exclusively through the Wyze app. Customizable Alerts: Set highly specific "motion zones" to reduce false notifications. Dream Hub (Momcozy) Dual-Stream Monitoring: Features a dedicated handheld monitor for a constant, lag-free feed that doesn't rely on your phone or Wi-Fi. Integrated Environment Tracking: Sensor data is displayed in real-time on both the handheld monitor and the app for immediate visibility.

chunks.text: The Cubby Bed and its technology accessories are designed to provide a customizable, safe environment that supports sensory regulation and improves sleep quality. chunks.pageTitle: What are the sensory features of the Cubby Bed?
chunks.text: The enclosed canopy serves as the foundation for creating a calming "sensory retreat": Light Control: The dual-fabric doors are made of heavy-duty mesh and durable fabric, allowing caregivers to customize light levels for a sleep-conducive environment. Sound Muffling: The canopy and heavy-duty mesh work together to limit outside noise that could disturb the child. Safe Padding: The canopy features tensioned, padded walls to prevent injury and provide a secure feeling of "cocooning".

chunks.pageTitle: What are the sensory features of the Cubby Bed?

chunks.pageTitle: What are the sensory features of the Cubby Bed?

Weighted Blanket: Provides deep pressure stimulation to increase serotonin and reduce nighttime movements. Vibration Pad: Offers proprioceptive input to help the user feel more grounded and reduce anxiety.

chunks.pageTitle: What are the features of the Cubby Bed that help with sleep?

chunks.pageTitle: How to work with appeals + denials
chunks.sectionTitle: How to work with appeals + denials
chunks.text: Did you receive a denial? Don’t be discouraged; we are here to help. If your insurance or Medicaid denied paying for a Cubby Bed, you can still try to get the specialized bed your loved one needs. We at Cubby are committed to helping you and your family. Below is information to guide you and show you the next steps you can take to get the bed. Your Medical Supplier will continue to be your best point of contact in getting a Cubby Bed through insurance/Medicaid. We recommend partnering with them to explore options such as resubmission, appeals or alternative pathways. They are experts in your state and local guidelines. For more information on the Medicaid Appeal Process, please click here. Here are a few things you can ask or tell your supplier to help guide the process: Ask what the next steps are available for resubmission or an appeal for your state and insurance plan. Share any updated medical documentation or Letters of Medical Necessity (LMN) from your provider. Confirm if there are specific forms, codes or additional information the insurer requires for a successful resubmission. Request a timeline for how long each step usually takes, so you know what to expect. Ask if there are alternative ways to move forward while waiting for insurance approval, such as financing or waivers. Things to keep in mind: Denials are not uncommon; families have found success in approvals after initially being denied. You will need a Medical Supplier to assist with the process, so it’s best to continue working with the same supplier. Suppliers are experts on state laws, policy requirements, and how best to navigate insurance and Medicaid processes. Common reasons for denial often involve documentation, such as the Letter of Medical Necessity (LMN). Ensure your provider is involved in preparing or updating documents for resubmission. The average time to process a resubmission or appeal is 1 to 2 months. Don’t get discouraged; these steps can be life-changing for your family. Alternative funding options: If you wish to explore routes beyond insurance/Medicaid, we provide information on waivers, charities, financing and private pay. View alternative funding options here 👈 For any questions, please reach out to our Cubby Care Team at hello@cubbymedical.com

chunks.pageTitle: Cubby Tech Hub & Dream Hub: Assembly and Installation Guide
chunks.text: Identify your model to follow the correct assembly path. The Technology Hub requires a quick manual internal setup, while the Dream Hub arrives fully assembled.

chunks.pageTitle: Cubby Tech Hub & Dream Hub: Assembly and Installation Guide
chunks.text: For Cubby 1: Technology Hub Assembly Open Hub: Place the unit face down and remove the five (5) black thumbscrews. Thread Cables: Feed the camera power cable through the designated hole in the back shell. Set Controls: Install either the Show Controls cover or the Hide Controls (blocker) over the light puck. Wiring Check: Ensure the radio antenna is coiled tightly and no wires are pinched. Seal Hub: Snap the shell closed and replace all 5 thumbscrews. Do not overtighten. For Cubby 2: Dream Hub Setup Digital Pairing: Power the unit near your router and follow the Momcozy App prompts to pair the light and camera. Monitor Sync: Turn on the handheld monitor; it will automatically find the Dream Hub camera signal. Placement Check: Ensure the camera lens is unobstructed within the secure enclosure before mounting.

chunks.pageTitle: Cubby Tech Hub & Dream Hub: Assembly and Installation Guide
chunks.sectionTitle: Installing the Hub into the Cubby Canopy
chunks.text: This process is the same for both models to ensure the safety of the enclosure: Insert & Zip: From the outside of the bed, insert the hub into the circular canopy portal and zip it into the track. Lock: Use the provided security lock to tether the zipper tab to the nylon safety loop. Power: Connect the power cords to a grounded outlet. Devices will auto-reconnect to their apps. CRITICAL SAFETY: Never leave the canopy portal open. If the hub is removed, you must install and lock the fabric hole cover immediately.

Download the application for your specific Cubby Bed model from Google Play or the App Store:

Cubby 1: Technology Hub Open Hub Case: Place the hub face down and remove the five (5) black thumbscrews. Lift the back shell and set it aside. Remove Camera: Gently take the camera out of the plastic casing. Power: Plug the camera into power using the included cable. A red light will appear once connected. Cubby 2: Dream Hub Internal Setup: The Dream Hub camera is pre-installed. Do not open the casing. Power: Plug the Dream Hub into a power outlet within range of your router. It will enter pairing mode automatically.

Open the Wyze app and tap + New Device > Cameras > Wyze Cam V3. Pull the base away from the camera body to find the Setup button on the bottom. Press the button until the camera says "Ready to Connect." Scan the QR code on your phone with the camera lens. Tap Next once you hear "QR code scanned." Open the Momcozy app, tap Add Device, and select the Baby Monitor (BM04) and the Sound Machine (DreamSync). Follow the on-screen prompts to connect to Wi-Fi. The app will automatically sync to your dashboard and handheld monitor.

Troubleshooting by Model

chunks.sectionTitle: Technology Hub: Philips Wake-Up Light Setup
chunks.text: If your Technology Hub light is not responding or showing the incorrect time, follow these steps to reset the connection: Step 1: App PreparationDownload the Philips SleepMapper App from Google Play or the App Store. Plug in your Philips Wake-Up Light; the screen should display "Hello." Step 2: Initiate PairingOpen the app and navigate to My Device > Connect a Device. Select Sleep & Wake-up Light and tap Get Started. Step 3: Device ConfigurationOn the physical Philips light, press the Play icon. Select your language and press the Check-Mark (✔️) to confirm. Ensure the Antenna Icon appears on the light's display. Step 4: Wi-Fi SyncIn the app, tap Connect to Sleep & Wake-Up Light. When prompted to join the network "PHILIPS Setup," select Join. Choose your home 2.4GHz Wi-Fi network, enter your password, and name your device. Note: If the connection fails, ensure your smartphone's operating system is fully up to date. A software update for the light may trigger automatically once connected.

The Dream Hub light is integrated directly into the bed's canopy and does not require an external app or Wi-Fi connection. Light Won't Turn On: Ensure the main power cable is securely plugged into the hub and the wall outlet. Check the "Light" button on the physical hub interface. Intensity Issues: Use the (+) and (-) buttons on the hub to adjust brightness levels. Color Malfunction: If the light is stuck on one color or flickering, perform a physical reset by unplugging the Dream Hub from the wall outlet for 60 seconds.

If you are experiencing issues with your camera feed or connectivity, most problems can be resolved by checking your Wi-Fi signal or restarting the hub. Note: The Cubby 2 Bed with Dream Hub began shipping out in Spring 2026. Orders fulfilled before this date included the Cubby 1 model. Additionally, you can easily identify your version by looking for an embroidered 'Cubby' logo on the front of the canopy (indicates Cubby 2) and/or a rainbow-shaped light on the hub (indicates the Dream Hub). For additional details about each model, click here.
Troubleshooting by Model

For Technology Hub (Wyze) The "Head-to-Foot" View: The camera is intentionally mounted sideways to capture the entire bed. If the image is sideways on your phone, simply enter Full Screen Mode to auto-rotate. Persistent Offline Status: If restarting the camera doesn't work, ensure your Wi-Fi password hasn't changed. Wyze cameras require a 2.4GHz signal; they will not connect to 5GHz-only networks. For Dream Hub (Momcozy) Monitor vs. App: If the handheld monitor works but the app doesn't, the issue is your home Wi-Fi. If neither works, the hub likely needs a restart. Handheld Monitor Lag: Ensure the monitor antenna is up. If the screen is black, check that the camera lens isn't obstructed by the safety enclosure. Status Lights: Check the LED on the Hub. A blinking light usually means it is searching for a Wi-Fi connection.

Check Volume: Confirm your phone’s media volume is up. Dream Hub users should also check the physical buttons on the handheld monitor. Permissions: Go to your phone's Settings > Apps > [Wyze or Momcozy] and ensure "Microphone" and "Nearby Devices" are toggled ON.

We want you to love your Cubby Bed, but we understand that every situation is unique. This guide outlines our industry-leading trial and return process. We stand behind our product with a 100-day trial and easy return policy for the bed.
We offer free return shipping and a full refund to make this decision as risk-free as possible. You have 100 days to decide at $0 cost to you (excludes AK + HI).

Accessories for both generations are returnable under our 100-day policy if they are unused and in original packaging: Cubby 1 (CB1): Includes the Technology Hub and original frame accessories. Cubby 2 (Dream Hub): Includes the Dream Hub electronics and updated hardware.

Submit Request: Complete the return form within 100 days of receiving your order. Receive Labels: Our Support Team will process your request and send free return shipping label(s). Drop-Off: Safely package the product in original packaging and drop it off with the carrier (FedEx or UPS). IMPORTANT: Returning without original packaging results in a 5% fee + repair costs for any damage. For international sales (outside continental US), shipping costs/duties are paid by the customer.

chunks.pageTitle: When will I receive my refund?
chunks.text: Inspection: All returns are subject to a thorough inspection at our warehouse before a refund is officially issued. Processing Time: Once the inspection is complete, your refund will be processed back to the original payment method. Estimated Arrival: Most refunds appear in your account within 5-7 business days after we receive the returned items. Note: Depending on your financial institution, it may take additional time for the credit to post to your statement after we have processed it on our end.

chunks.pageTitle: My order arrived damaged. What do I do?
chunks.sectionTitle: My order arrived damaged. What do I do?
chunks.text: We are incredibly sorry if your order arrived in less-than-perfect condition. While we take every precaution in packaging, shipping mishaps can occasionally occur. If your Cubby Bed frame, canopy, or electronics (Tech Hub/Dream Hub) were damaged during transit, we will make it right immediately.

chunks.pageTitle: My order arrived damaged. What do I do?
chunks.text: To help our team resolve this for you as quickly as possible, please follow these steps: Document the Damage: Take clear photos of the damaged item and the exterior packaging/box. Contact Support: Email our team at support@cubbybeds.com. Include Details: Provide your Order Number and a brief description of the affected parts (e.g., "Cubby 2 Frame Box A" or "Dream Hub Power Cord"). SAFETY CHECK: If a structural component of the frame or a safety zipper is damaged, do not complete assembly until you have spoken with a Support representative.
This article will provide details on current shipping timelines, regional availability, and a breakdown of what to expect when your Cubby Bed shipment arrives.

We currently ship to all 50 states using FedEx Ground delivery. Please note that Alaska and Hawaii are the only states exempt from our free shipping policy. Processing Time: Our lead time is approximately 1-3 business days. Transit Time: Once shipped from Denver, Colorado, the estimated delivery timeframe is 3-5 business days (depending on the destination address). Tracking: You will receive FedEx order tracking information via email as soon as your order leaves our warehouse.

chunks.sectionTitle: What comes in the Cubby Bed shipment?
chunks.text: To ensure safe delivery, your order is split into multiple boxes. Reviews the model details below:

This article explains our current international shipping availability and provides a way to be notified when shipping becomes available in other regions. Currently, we can only ship directly to the consumer within the United States due to the medical regulations around the product. If you would like to be notified when shipping is available to your country, please complete this form.

chunks.pageTitle: Is there a warranty on your products?
chunks.sectionTitle: Is there a warranty on your products?
chunks.text: Yes — we proudly stand behind our products! Please read our Warranty Policy for more information.

This article provides the direct link to register your Cubby Bed warranty and outlines the various ways we are available to support your family. Thank you for choosing a Cubby Bed! Please click here to register your Cubby Bed's warranty. We are so happy that you've joined our Cubby family! We look forward to assisting you with: Guiding you on features of the Cubby Bed Questions on setup/assembly Supporting you in any replacements needed

chunks.pageTitle: What should I do if my Cubby Bed is damaged or defective? How do I get a replacement?
chunks.sectionTitle: What should I do if my Cubby Bed is damaged or defective? How do I get a replacement?
chunks.text: This article explains how to report damaged or defective components, the information required to expedite a solution, and how to determine if a replacement is covered by warranty or insurance. Important Warning: Cubby Beds does not recommend the continued use of a product that has been damaged. We recommend that you stop using the damaged product immediately and make alternate sleeping arrangements until a replacement can be arranged.

chunks.pageTitle: What should I do if my Cubby Bed is damaged or defective? How do I get a replacement?
chunks.text: To report a damaged or defective item, please start by completing the Contact Us Form on our website. This form will walk you through a set of questions to help us expedite a solution. Required Information Includes: Where exactly the damage is located and how it occurred. Your original Order Number (or PO# if purchased via a medical supplier). At least one (1) photo of the damage.

chunks.pageTitle: What should I do if my Cubby Bed is damaged or defective? How do I get a replacement?
chunks.text: Replacement items may be covered under our Warranty Policy. If the damage is not covered by warranty, it may still be eligible for insurance coverage; we can coordinate with your medical supplier to submit a quote. Response Time: Our Support team typically responds within 1 business day of form submission. Note: All warranty claims are subject to inspection by Cubby Beds. We reserve the right to determine coverage at our sole discretion.

chunks.pageTitle: I completed my warranty registration. When can I expect my gift card?
chunks.sectionTitle: I completed my warranty registration. When can I expect my gift card?
chunks.text: This article shares information about the processing times for Warranty Registrations and what to expect after your submission has been reviewed. Please allow 7-10 business days to process your Warranty Registration once it has been submitted to our team. This process can be delayed if your registration does not include a valid order number. Click here to learn how to find your Order Number, Purchase Order (PO) Number, or Lot code. Once we locate your original order, we'll complete your registration and send you a confirmation email to let you know that your gift card is on the way. You can expect to receive the gift card via email within 24 hours of the confirmation email. If you don't see it in your inbox, check your Spam folder. If you're unable to locate it after 24 hours, let us know and we'll re-send it to you.

chunks.pageTitle: How can I get help? What is your phone number or email address?
chunks.sectionTitle: How can I get help? What is your phone number or email address?
chunks.text: This article provides the various ways to contact the Cubby Support Team, including our phone number, email address, and standard business hours.

chunks.pageTitle: How can I get help? What is your phone number or email address?
chunks.text: The Cubby Beds Support Team can be reached by Phone, Email, or Text message during business hours: Phone: 855-964-2664 Text message: 855-964-3824 Email: support@cubbybeds.com Standard Business Hours: Monday through Friday: 9:00 am - 5:00 pm Mountain Time Note: Cubby offices are closed on major holidays.

chunks.pageTitle: How do I find my Order Number, Purchase Order (PO) Number, or Lot Code?
chunks.sectionTitle: How do I find my Order Number, Purchase Order (PO) Number, or Lot Code?
chunks.text: This article describes how to find your Order Number or Purchase Order (PO) number, as well as the Lot code located on your canopy label.

chunks.pageTitle: How do I find my Order Number, Purchase Order (PO) Number, or Lot Code?
chunks.text: Please check your email for a confirmation message containing your order number. Our team can also look up this information using the email address associated with the order. Alternatively, the lot/serial # is found on the underside of the Warnings & Precautions Label on the canopy of your bed.

chunks.pageTitle: How do I find my Order Number, Purchase Order (PO) Number, or Lot Code?
chunks.text: If you purchased your Cubby Bed through insurance, you should contact your DME (Durable Medical Equipment supplier) for your Purchase Order (PO) number. You can also: Check Delivery Paperwork: Review the documents received from the DME at the time of delivery; it should list an "Order #" or "Purchase Order #." Provider Details: If paperwork is missing, we can try to locate the order using the name and branch location (city/state) of your medical supplier. Note: If you are still unable to find your order information, please reach out to our team at support@cubbybeds.com for assistance.

Your Cubby Bed camera can listen for the specific frequencies of standard smoke and carbon monoxide (CO) alarms to send you a push notification if they go off. Follow the steps below for your specific hub. Note: The Cubby 2 Bed with Dream Hub began shipping out in Spring 2026. Orders fulfilled before this date included the Cubby 1 model. Additionally, you can easily identify your version by looking for an embroidered 'Cubby' logo on the front of the canopy (indicates Cubby 2) and/or a rainbow-shaped light on the hub (indicates the Dream Hub). For additional details about each model, click here.
Troubleshooting by Model

To enable these alerts on the Technology Hub, follow these steps within the app: Open the Wyze App and select your Cubby camera from the home screen. Tap Settings (the gear icon) in the top right corner. Select Alarm Settings from the menu. Toggle Alerts: Switch both Smoke Alarm Sound and CO Alarm Sound to ON.

To ensure you receive notifications on both your phone and the handheld monitor: Open the Momcozy App and select your hub. Access Settings: Tap the settings icon to view camera and sound options. Select Detection Settings: Look for "Sound Detection" or "Alarm Alerts." Enable Alerts: Ensure the toggles for Smoke and CO Alarm Sound are enabled. Check Handheld Monitor: Ensure the monitor is not on "Mute" so you can hear the audible alarm from the hub.

Want to know more about how charities help in funding a Cubby Bed? This article will discuss all you need to know about charities and how to successfully work with them. Included in this article are the following: How does grant funding work through charities? What charities have Cubby Beds worked with? How can I find a charity? What are the things to consider when reaching out to charities? Alternative fundraising Ideas

chunks.sectionTitle: How does grant funding work through charities?
chunks.text: Typically, grants are funds given by government agencies, foundations, or other organizations to fund specific projects, programs, or activities. They are usually given to nonprofit organizations like charities and may be used to support research, education, special needs communities, etc. Charities are whom we can partner with and it is best to reach out to them to assist you in funding a Cubby Bed!

chunks.sectionTitle: What charities have Cubby Beds worked with? How can I find a charity?
chunks.text: Great news! Locating a charity that you can work with is EASY! To get started, please refer to the list of charities below that we have successfully worked with or been recommended. Simply click on the link for each charity to learn more about their services and contact information. ✅ Accessia Healthcare - Helps cover medical bills/fees ✅ Angelman Syndrome Foundation- Nationwide foundation that helps children with Angelman syndrome (AS) is a rare neuro-genetic disorder ✅ Apply for Help - Modest Needs® grant ✅Great Nonprofits- Search by city and state ✅Kids Mobility -based in Denver but serves the US ✅ MEC Disabilities - Apply for Adaptive Medical & Nonmedical Equipment for anyone 21 years of age or younger residing in the United States ✅My Gym Foundation- a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit dedicated to helping children with disabilities and those struggling financially by awarding critically needed gifts of equipment and services ✅ Sunshine Foundation -Sunshine Foundation answers the dreams of children, ages 3-18, who have severe or profound physical/developmental/intellectual challenges or trauma from physical/sexual abuse, and whose families have limited income. ✅ UHCCF- Their mission is to significantly enhance the clinical condition or quality of life of children covered under a commercial health insurance plan and who are 16 years old or younger living in the United States. ✅ HealthWell Foundation- Reduce financial barriers to care for underinsured patients with chronic or life-altering diseases ✅ The Arc - 730 state and local chapters. Works in coalition with other disability, civil rights, and human services groups. ✅ United Cerebral Palsy - Promotes independence and inclusion of people with cerebral palsy, intellectual, developmental, and other disabilities through a network of affiliates and partnerships. ⭐️ An additional resource for caregivers please click here → Parent to Parent. Their mission is to support the national network of Parent-to-Parent programs that provide access to emotional support for families of individuals with disabilities and/or special healthcare needs.

chunks.sectionTitle: What are the things to consider when reaching out to charities?
chunks.text: ✅ Do your research: Be sure to record helpful information for your partner charity. This includes application guidelines and required documents for fund approval. ✅ Call or Email: It's a good idea to call or email the appropriate contact person for the most up-to-date information on funding guidelines and application information. ✅ Follow Instructions: Be sure to follow any instructions closely, especially deadlines. Charities typically require a quote or an invoice from us. We’d be glad to provide you with those documents. Send us an email at hello@cubbybeds.com.

Have some challenges working with charities? We got you! If the option of reaching out to charities won’t work, there are several ways in which you can raise funds for a Cubby Bed. Please see below for some effective fundraising ideas. ✅ Church donations: Speak to your pastor or church volunteer about what is needed for you to raise a fund. You can suggest unique ideas such as pledges, fundraising letters, etc. One local church in Bellingham, WA — Christ the King Community Church — has successfully helped a family fund a Cubby Bed. ✅ Local fundraising efforts: A variety of fundraising activities that you can do include book sales, t-shirt sales, board game tournaments, charity theater events, local concerts, etc. ✅ GoFundMe - It is an online fundraising platform for individuals and groups. You create a campaign to tell your story and set a fundraising goal. Then, share your campaign on social media and accept donations through the platform. You can learn more by visiting their website.

Would you like to know more about how waiver funding works for a Cubby Bed? This article will help you understand waivers and how to successfully fund a Cubby Bed using a waiver. Included in this article are the following: What are waivers? How can I find a waiver in my state? How can I apply for a waiver? How can I apply for waiver funds to get a Cubby?

Aside from charities, waivers are an alternative funding option that can help you fund a Cubby Bed. Waivers are funding programs that are available in each US state and allow your loved one to receive products or services that are not traditionally covered by your insurance/Medicaid. This includes safety beds like the Cubby Bed! The good news is that Cubby Beds have been covered and paid for by Medicaid waivers in 20+ states! 🎉

chunks.sectionTitle: How can I find a waiver in my state?
chunks.text: Having some challenges finding a waiver in your state? Not a problem! Listed below are a couple of resources that can help you locate a waiver within your state. Click on the links below to get started. ✅ MedicaidWaiver.org - This website will display available waivers in each state. This will also display qualification guidelines, services offered, and limitations for each waiver. ✅ KidsWaivers.org - Scroll to the middle of the page to select your state. Once you select your state, you will see each waiver's details by clicking the '[+]'. These waivers are also great to cover products and services that are typically denied by insurance/Medicaid.

chunks.sectionTitle: How can I apply for a waiver?
chunks.text: Once you have successfully found a waiver program within your state, check the application guidelines on their website. This will provide you with a step-by-step guide on what information and documents are needed to apply for a waiver. If the information is not available, it is best to reach out to them via phone call or email to gather as much information as possible. As a reminder, the application process for a waiver will vary depending on the specific waiver that you’re applying for and the state in which you reside.

chunks.sectionTitle: I got approved for waiver funds. How can I apply this fund to get a Cubby?
chunks.text: The Cubby Care Team would love to assist you in the funding process! Every waiver has a different funding pathway. To get the ball rolling, we need to confirm the following information below: ✅ Is your waiver under the category of assistive technology, specialized medical equipment, home modifications, or safety equipment? ✅ Is it required to have a ‘denial’ from your insurance/Medicaid before funds can be applied? This is very common. If this information is not available, please reach out to your case manager/service coordinator. And once we have determined your waiver coverage, we can now help you decide the products that are best for your loved one. We'd be glad to provide you with a quote or invoice from us — just let us know! If you or your case manager have any questions, reach out anytime! Send us an email at hello@cubbybeds.com.

Finding the right medical supplier is a major step in the insurance/Medicaid journey. Let's discuss how best to find a Medical Supplier to assist you in pursuing coverage for a Cubby Bed. Using the Cubby Supplier Locator Our Supplier Locator is a great place to start! It’s a tool to help families start their search for a Medical Supplier. Click here to try it out! Search by Location: Enter your Zip Code or City/State and hit enter. Zoom Out: Many suppliers service a radius of up to 100 miles. If you don’t see a match immediately, use the +/- buttons on the map to expand your search. Stay In-State: If you are using Medicaid, most programs require you to work with a supplier located within the same state.

chunks.sectionTitle: Once you find a Medical Supplier on our list:
chunks.text: We recommend that you call the branches located nearest you and ask the following: ✅ Do you provide safety beds like the Cubby Bed? ✅ Ask if the supplier accepts your primary and/or secondary insurance/Medicaid. If the answer is yes to both, collect their fax, email, and phone information. This is where your required documents will be sent once you have requested them from your providers. If you're unable to find a supplier on our locator: If you are unsuccessful in getting connected with a supplier on our locator to assist you on your journey, keep going! Please consider the below options for finding a Medical Supplier: Doctor Referral: Your loved one’s doctor may have a supplier they refer out to, in which case they may fax those documents over to the supplier. Not every practice has experience referring out for a safety bed, but it's always worth asking! Insurance Referral: Ask your insurance for a list of approved, in-network Medical Equipment Suppliers near you. Please contact these suppliers to confirm they can provide Cubby Beds. Additionally, you may cross-reference your insurance’s list with our Supplier Locator to help identify contracted Medical Suppliers in your area. New Medical Suppliers: We can work with any supplier, not just those on our website! At Cubby Beds, we can work with other suppliers as long as: ✅ They can supply safety beds, such as a Cubby Bed ✅ Accept your primary and/or secondary insurance/Medicaid If the answer is yes to both, collect their fax, email, and phone information, please have them complete our ‘Become a Supplier’ form to get set up quickly and easily to distribute Cubby Beds.

chunks.pageTitle: Acronyms you may need to know
chunks.sectionTitle: Acronyms you may need to know
chunks.text: There are several organizations and acronyms that you will encounter throughout the Insurance/Medicaid process, such as the Durable Medical Equipment Supplier (DME) you will need to partner with for your submission to insurance. We understand these medical terms, acronyms, and organizations can get confusing. To help, below is a quick guide on some acronyms that can guide you throughout your Cubby journey:

chunks.pageTitle: Acronyms you may need to know
chunks.text: OT = Occupational Therapist PT = Physical Therapist PCP = Primary Care Provider HCPCS = Healthcare Common Procedure Coding System (Insurance Billing Codes for Cubby Safety Beds and Products) ICD = Diagnosis Codes for Insurance DME = Durable Medical Equipment (Cubby Safety Beds) or Durable Medical Equipment Supplier HME = Home Medical Equipment ATP = Assistive Technology Professional, from a DME PO = Purchase Order/Product Order (Order# from DME to Cubby Beds) LMN = Letter of Medical Necessity LCA = Less Costly Alternatives Rx = Prescription

We’re excited you’re interested in the Cubby Bed! The first step to getting your loved one a safe night’s sleep is to understand your options. The Cubby Bed is insurance/Medicaid eligible, but many other funding options are also available.To help, we've put together a step-by-step Insurance Guide that explains the steps for insurance coverage.

chunks.sectionTitle: Click here to see the step-by-step insurance process guide!
chunks.text: If you're looking for alternative funding options, we also have information about that here.

chunks.pageTitle: What is a Case Manager and How Can They Help You Get a Cubby Bed?
chunks.sectionTitle: What is a Case Manager and How Can They Help You Get a Cubby Bed?
chunks.text: If you have Medicaid or a Medicaid waiver, you may have a case manager—a healthcare professional who helps coordinate services and support for families with complex medical needs. While case managers can be a valuable resource, their availability and level of involvement vary. You’ll still need to take an active role in the process, but they may be able to provide guidance and assistance along the way.

chunks.pageTitle: What is a Case Manager and How Can They Help You Get a Cubby Bed?
chunks.sectionTitle: Ways a Case Manager May Be Able to Help
chunks.text: Guidance on PaperworkCase managers can help you understand what’s needed, like a prescription and Letter of Medical Necessity (LMN). Connecting You to a Medical SupplierThey may be aware of local suppliers that accept Medicaid and can point you in the right direction. Advocacy & General SupportCase managers often work to ensure families receive the services and equipment they need, though they may be balancing a high number of cases so availability varies.

chunks.pageTitle: What is a Case Manager and How Can They Help You Get a Cubby Bed?
chunks.sectionTitle: How to Find Out if You Have a Case Manager
chunks.text: Call Your Medicaid ProviderAsk if your loved one has an assigned case manager or can be assigned one. Ask Your Healthcare ProviderYour doctor or therapist may know if you have one. Contact Your State’s Disability ServicesIf your loved one is in a Medicaid waiver program, they will have a case manager. This case manager will typically conduct an initial evaluation to determine the level of care needed, and provide guidance on support and services your loved one can receive. While a case manager can be a helpful resource, securing a Cubby Bed is still a process that requires your involvement. If you're unsure who your case manager is or if you are eligible for one, please reach out to your local Medicaid agency.

chunks.pageTitle: The role of Cubby Beds, medical suppliers, and your insurance company
chunks.sectionTitle: The role of Cubby Beds, medical suppliers, and your insurance company
chunks.text: Throughout your insurance/Medicaid journey, there will be at least three organizations you will be working with. These include your (1) health insurance company or companies, (2) a durable medical equipment (DME) supplier, and (3) Cubby Beds. Below is an explanation of each organization.

chunks.pageTitle: The role of Cubby Beds, medical suppliers, and your insurance company
chunks.text: Health insurance companies play a vital role in the healthcare system by providing coverage for medical expenses. These companies offer a range of health insurance plans that individuals, families, and businesses can purchase to help cover the costs of healthcare services and durable medical equipment such as safety beds, which includes the Cubby Bed!

chunks.pageTitle: The role of Cubby Beds, medical suppliers, and your insurance company
chunks.text: Durable medical equipment (DME) suppliers play an important role in the healthcare system by providing equipment and supplies that are necessary for the treatment and management of medical conditions. DME includes a wide range of products, such as wheelchairs, crutches, CPAP machines, and safety beds like Cubby Beds. We work with hundreds of DME suppliers across the country to assist families and bill their Insurance/Medicaid for their Durable Medical Equipment requests. They are responsible for packaging all required documents to submit to your insurance/Medicaid, such as the prescription and the Letter of Medical Necessity (LMN).

chunks.pageTitle: The role of Cubby Beds, medical suppliers, and your insurance company
chunks.text: Hey, that's us! Cubby Beds are the manufacturer of these safety beds with a mission of providing families with better sleep and safer nights. We are here to guide you if you have any questions or meet any roadblocks along your Cubby journey!

chunks.pageTitle: The role of Cubby Beds, medical suppliers, and your insurance company
chunks.text: Funding a Cubby Bed through insurance/Medicaid often requires several people working together. The organizations we talked about earlier are not the only ones you might deal with to make sure your loved one gets enough sleep. Other professionals, like Case Managers, are very important people who can help you throughout the insurance process. If you want more details about Case Managers, you can check out our article here.

chunks.pageTitle: Getting a Cubby Bed Prescription and LMN
chunks.sectionTitle: Getting a Cubby Bed Prescription and LMN
chunks.text: Having a plan and communicating effectively with your loved one’s medical team is the first step to advocating for their needs. The steps in this article will prepare you for your next appointment with your doctor and how to talk about the Cubby Bed for your loved one.

chunks.pageTitle: Getting a Cubby Bed Prescription and LMN
chunks.sectionTitle: Step 1: Decide which providers you’ll need for the required documents
chunks.text: At least two documents are required nationwide to submit to insurance/Medicaid for the Cubby Bed. 1) a Prescription, and 2) a Letter of Medical Necessity The prescription must be written by a prescribing physician. Decide which doctor you would like to write the 'Cubby Safety Bed' prescription. Ideally this would be the prescribing physician that is most familiar with your loved one’s safety concerns, diagnoses, and medical needs. Decide which provider you would like to write the Letter of Medical Necessity (LMN). Insurance typically prefers to see Letters of Medical Necessity (LMN) from an occupational therapist (OT), physical therapist (PT), or Neurologist. If your loved one does not see these professionals, the prescribing doctor may also be able to write the LMN. Click here for state-by-state information on who can author your LMN.

chunks.pageTitle: Getting a Cubby Bed Prescription and LMN
chunks.text: Call your doctor's office to make an appointment to talk about requesting Durable Medical Equipment such as the Cubby Bed. If you’re following our Cubby Guide Anna’s emails, be certain to share the appointment date with us. If you would like to receive this guided support, please take our Funding Quiz.

chunks.pageTitle: Getting a Cubby Bed Prescription and LMN
chunks.text: Please download and print our Required Insurance Documents. Share these documents with your doctor ahead of the appointment to streamline the process. Before your appointment, we recommend completing our optional Safety Needs & Concerns Worksheet. This not only can be helpful in having a list of concerns to discuss with your doctor, it recommended you request it is included in your loved one’s medical chart to ensure these needs/concerns are documented for the future.

chunks.pageTitle: Getting a Cubby Bed Prescription and LMN
chunks.text: During your appointment, make sure to go through the following steps: Share the Required Insurance Documents with your loved one’s doctor, if you have not already. Explain your loved one's safety concerns, what you have tried to address those concerns, and how the Cubby Bed can help. Request a prescription (Rx) specifically for a 'Cubby Safety Bed' by providing the Rx Form within the required documents download. Make sure to share the Durable Medical Equipment (DME) Supplier's contact information with your doctor for easy communication, as your doctor can send these documents directly to the DME. Need help finding a DME supplier? Check out our Supplier Locator Map here. Request a prescription (Rx) specifically for a 'Cubby Safety Bed' by providing the Rx Form within the required documents download. Make sure to share the Durable Medical Equipment (DME) Supplier's contact information with your doctor for easy communication, as your doctor can send these documents directly to the DME. Need help finding a DME supplier? Check out our Supplier Locator Map here. If the prescribing doctor will also be writing the LMN, kindly request a Letter of Medical Necessity (LMN). Important note: Please ensure that your doctor's Rx specifies a 'Cubby Safety Bed'. Otherwise, insurance/Medicaid may deny your request due to other less costly alternatives being available. Lastly, we are here to help you throughout the insurance/Medicaid process! If you have questions, please email our Cubby Care Team at hello@cubbymedical.com

chunks.pageTitle: Letter of Medical Necessity 101: Everything You Need to Know
chunks.sectionTitle: Letter of Medical Necessity 101: Everything You Need to Know
chunks.text: We know the process to receive insurance/Medicaid approval can feel overwhelming, but we're here to help you every step of the way! One of the most important tools in your journey to getting a Cubby Bed for your loved one is the Letter of Medical Necessity (LMN). What's an LMN? Well here is the exact definition: “A Letter of Medical Necessity (LMN) is the written explanation from the treating physician describing the medical need for services, equipment, or supplies to assist the claimant in the treatment, care, or relief of their accepted work-related illness(es). … Medical Benefit Examiners (MBE) will weigh all evidence on file to determine if the medical evidence establishes medical necessity for the request.” - U.S. Department of Labor (.gov)In other words, it's a powerful document written by your loved one's prescribing doctor, physical therapist, or occupational therapist that explains why the Cubby Bed is medically necessary for your loved one's well-being. Think of it as your loved one's story, told through a medical lens. Why is an LMN so important? Your LMN is like a persuasive essay to your insurance company. It helps them understand: The unique sleep and safety challenges your loved one faces Other solutions you've already tried that haven't quite worked out How the Cubby Bed's special features can make a real difference in your loved one's life To make this process as smooth as possible, we've put together a super helpful LMN Guide. It's packed with everything you need: An easy-to-follow LMN outline (perfect for your provider!) Justification guides that highlight how our Cubby Bed can help Answers to all those burning questions you might have about LMNs Ready to dive in? Access our complete LMN Guide here! Remember, you're not alone in this! Looking for guided support? Take our Funding Quiz to receive step-by-step instructions on how to pursue insurance/Medicaid coverage. We're rooting for you and your family!

chunks.pageTitle: How to Purchase Accessories for Your Cubby Bed
chunks.sectionTitle: How to Purchase Accessories for Your Cubby Bed
chunks.text: To help you get the most out of your Cubby ecosystem, we offer a range of accessories designed for safety, sensory support, and comfort. All accessories can be purchased directly from our website. Depending on medical necessity, certain items may also be covered by your insurance provider.

chunks.pageTitle: How to Purchase Accessories for Your Cubby Bed
chunks.text: If medically necessary, your insurance may cover specific accessories. To explore this, contact your Medical Supplier (DME) directly. They will navigate your benefits and guide you through their specific ordering procedures.

chunks.pageTitle: How to Purchase Accessories for Your Cubby Bed
chunks.text: When ordering, ensure you select accessories compatible with your specific model: Cubby 1 (CB1): Uses the Technology Hub. If upgrading to the Dream Hub, ensure you follow the "Dream Hub for Cubby 1" installation guide. Cubby 2: Arrives with the Dream Hub. All "Dream Hub" branded accessories are fully compatible.

chunks.pageTitle: How to Purchase Accessories for Your Cubby Bed
chunks.text: For the quickest delivery, the following items are available for immediate checkout: Electronics: Dream Hub, Vibration Pad. Comfort: Weighted Blankets, Positioning Wedges, Mattress Protectors. Hardware: Lockable Wheels Kit, Floor Anchor Sets. PRO TIP: To purchase directly, visit the Accessories section of our website. Simply add items to your cart and proceed to checkout.

chunks.pageTitle: Who Would Benefit from a Cubby Bed?
chunks.sectionTitle: Who Would Benefit from a Cubby Bed?
chunks.text: Who would benefit from a Cubby Bed? The Cubby Bed was created for children with complex needs to provide safer sleep and sensory support.This article includes: WHO may qualify WHY it’s a necessity WHAT is needed to request Tips for Success

chunks.pageTitle: Who Would Benefit from a Cubby Bed?
chunks.sectionTitle: Do You Need a Diagnosis to Qualify for a Safety Bed?
chunks.text: The Cubby Bed is a medical device. To qualify for a Cubby Bed through insurance, you will need a documented medical condition or functional impairment tied to safety concerns. Some examples of common diagnoses for our users are: ADHD with severe impulsivity or sleep disruption Angelman syndrome Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) Cerebral palsy Epilepsy/seizure disorders Down syndrome Intellectual or developmental disabilities (IDD) Rett syndrome Sensory Processing Disorder (with documented safety concerns) Traumatic brain injury (TBI)

chunks.pageTitle: Who Would Benefit from a Cubby Bed?
chunks.text: When applying for insurance, you’ll likely hear the term "Medical Necessity." This simply means an individual must demonstrate clear safety risks and behaviors for an insurance company to consider the bed medically necessary. Below are examples of safety concerns the Cubby Bed can address: Aggressive or destructive behaviors: Outbursts that may involve hitting, kicking, throwing objects, or damaging furniture during sleep transitions that require a contained environment to protect both the child and caregivers. Cognitive Safety Deficits: An inability to recognize or respond to environmental dangers independently during the night. Entrapment & Burrowing: The risk of becoming wedged in gaps or stuck under bedding, which can lead to restricted breathing or an inability to self-rescue. Fall Risk: Falls due to mobility issues, coordination disorders, medication side effects, or vision impairment that could lead to injury. PICA and Eating of Non-food Items: The tendency to mouth or ingest non-food items, including traditional bedding or foam, which poses a significant safety hazard. Seizure Activity: Risk of injury from hitting hard surfaces, falling during a seizure, or becoming entraped in standard bed rails, requiring a soft environment and consistent monitoring. Self-Injurious Behaviors: Sensory-seeking behaviors such as head-banging or biting that require a protected sleep environment. Sensory Processing Difficulties: Hypersensitivity and/or overstimulation to light, sound, or textures that disrupts sleep and requires a controlled, calming environment to prevent unsafe behaviors. Sleep Disturbances: Challenges falling asleep, staying asleep, and night awakenings. Wandering & Elopement: Leaving the safety of the bed/home/room without an awareness of danger, often while caregivers are asleep. It must be documented why less costly options are inappropriate or have not worked in the past to address safety risk. Essentially, they need to explain how a Cubby Bed addresses safety concerns that less costly options—like a regular bed—cannot. Some common, less costly alternatives are: Alternative safety beds/bed tents Baby Monitors Bed/room modifications Bed alarms Behavioral Therapy Baby gates Helmet Locks on doors Mattress on the floor Medication Sound machines Sensory and soothing bedtime routines Please note, this is not an exhaustive list. Any alternatives tried can help your case.

chunks.pageTitle: Who Would Benefit from a Cubby Bed?
chunks.text: A Cubby Bed is medical equipment. Due to this, insurance payors require documentation from medical providers to understand the medical need for a patient. To begin, you will need the following: A prescription from a healthcare provider and a letter of medical necessity, preferably from an OT/PT. Please click here to view your state requirements. Detailed notes describing the child’s specific safety risks and behaviors Supporting documentation (such as therapy notes or caregiver observations, when available) Your Medical Supplier can inform you of any additional documentation required according to your specific insurance policy.

chunks.pageTitle: Who Would Benefit from a Cubby Bed?
chunks.text: Stronger documentation can help improve your chances of approval. Be sure to include the following: Your loved one’s condition or behaviors → the safety concerns they exhibit → how a Cubby Bed can keep them safe To assist your provider with thorough documentation, we strongly recommend our Safety & Concerns Worksheet. As someone who spends the most time with your loved one, you can speak specifically to safety concerns they exhibit, what you have previously tried that did not work, and what features of the Cubby Bed they can benefit from.

chunks.pageTitle: Who Would Benefit from a Cubby Bed?
chunks.text: Navigating the insurance process for a Cubby Bed can feel overwhelming, but you don’t have to do it alone. Our team is here to help guide you through documentation, next steps, and the overall process of securing a safer sleep solution for your loved one. If you have questions about your specific situation, reach out by clicking here—we’re happy to help.

chunks.pageTitle: Moving or Disassembling the Cubby Bed
chunks.sectionTitle: Moving or Disassembling the Cubby Bed
chunks.text: The Cubby Bed is designed for maximum stability and safety. Due to its sturdy construction and width, it typically requires two adults to reposition within a room or partial disassembly to move between rooms.

chunks.pageTitle: Moving or Disassembling the Cubby Bed
chunks.text: Moving within a room: Two adults can safely lift and reposition the bed; or, if optional wheels are installed, one adult can unlock the wheels, reposition the bed, and relock the wheels. Moving to a new room or home: We recommend fully disassembling the Cubby Bed and reassembling it in the new location to prevent damage.

chunks.pageTitle: Moving or Disassembling the Cubby Bed
chunks.text: If your family requires frequent movement of the bed, we offer optional wheels for the feet of the Cubby Bed. These wheels include locking brakes to ensure the bed remains secure when stationary.

This article outlines the industry-standard encryption, secure cloud hosting, and local privacy features used to protect your family's data across both the Technology Hub and Dream Hub systems. If your Hub has a round light and requires assembly, you have the Technology Hub; if it has a rainbow-shaped light and arrives pre-assembled, you have the Dream Hub. Note: The Dream Hub began shipping in Spring 2026. Orders fulfilled before this date included the Technology Hub model.

Our security standards are consistent across all Cubby Bed systems to ensure your data remains private: Cloud Provider: Both systems utilize AWS (Amazon Web Services) for secure, reliable data hosting. Encryption Standard: All video and personal data are protected by AES 128-bit encryption. Authentication: We use a TLS Handshake with unique secret keys for every device to validate identity and prevent unauthorized access.

Whichever hub you are using, your video stream is protected by multiple layers of security: Encryption at Rest and in Transit: Communication between your mobile device, your camera, and the AWS Cloud Server is made via HTTPS/TLS. This ensures alert videos remain private while traveling across the internet. Military-Grade AES Encryption: Live stream and playback data are encrypted via AES 128-bit encryption. Even if intercepted, data cannot be decrypted without the specific secret keys. Secure Identity Validation: We use a "handshake" process to validate the identity of your specific camera and smartphone before any data is shared.

The Dream Hub offers an additional layer of privacy. The connection between the handheld monitor and the hub uses a secure, closed-circuit radio frequency that does not require the internet to function, providing a private local loop in addition to the encrypted Wi-Fi app stream. The Momcozy 5-Inch Dual-mode Smart Baby Monitor (BM04) is fully integrated into our Dream Hub system. For more information regarding their specific security precautions, you can view the official Momcozy help center article here: 5-Inch Dual-mode Smart Baby Monitor-BM04
Accidents are a part of life, and the Cubby Bed is designed to handle them. The safety sheets are machine-washable, and the canopy fabric is designed for easy spot-cleaning.

To prolong the life of your mattress and make cleanup even easier, we offer a specialized mattress protector as an additional accessory. This creates an extra layer of defense against moisture and allergens. View the Cubby Mattress Protector

chunks.sectionTitle: Cleaning the Cubby Bed After an Accident
chunks.text: Safety Sheets: Our innovative microfiber zipper sheets are fully machine-washable. We recommend keeping a spare set on hand for quick changes during the night. Canopy Side Walls: If the side walls are affected, the best approach is to spot clean immediately. Blot (do not rub) the area with a clean, dry cloth to soak up as much liquid as possible.
For detailed deep-cleaning steps, see our guide on Cleaning & Washing Your Cubby Bed Canopy.

chunks.sectionTitle: Cubby Beds Materials and Safety Standards
chunks.text: The Cubby Bed is engineered for long-term durability and safety, using high-quality steel, chemical-free fabrics, and industry-leading electronics.

chunks.pageTitle: Cubby Beds Materials and Safety Standards
chunks.text: The Cubby Bed frame is constructed from galvanized and powder-coated steel. This ensures long-term structural integrity, resistance to wear, and a stable environment for your child.

chunks.pageTitle: Cubby Beds Materials and Safety Standards
chunks.text: The Cubby Bed enclosure is made from solution-dyed acrylic, chosen for being both ultra-tough and stain-resistant. We prioritize your loved one’s health; therefore, no chemical flame retardants are added to our fabrics.

chunks.pageTitle: Cubby Beds Materials and Safety Standards
chunks.text: Despite being chemical-free, our fabric meets or exceeds the following rigorous flame retardancy standards: California Technical Bulletin 117-2013 Class 1 NFPA 260:2013 Class 1 UFAC 1990

chunks.pageTitle: Cleaning and Washing Your Cubby Bed Canopy
chunks.sectionTitle: Cleaning and Washing Your Cubby Bed Canopy
chunks.text: To maintain the quality of your Cubby Bed canopy, we recommend spot-cleaning for minor spills or hand-washing for deeper cleans. The fabric is machine-washable for larger messes when specific care steps are followed.

chunks.pageTitle: Cleaning and Washing Your Cubby Bed Canopy
chunks.text: Accidents happen, but the Cubby Bed canopy is designed with tough, stain-fighting properties. For new spills: Liquid Spills: Blot (do not rub) the liquid with a clean, dry cloth immediately. Oil-Based Spills: Apply an absorbent powder such as cornstarch to the area, then remove it with a straight edge.

chunks.pageTitle: Cleaning and Washing Your Cubby Bed Canopy
chunks.text: If a spill requires more than a blot, follow these steps to prevent the stain from setting: Solution: Mix a mild cleaning solution of water and a gentle soap (such as Dawn or Woolite). Agitation: Spray the solution onto the fabric and use a sponge or soft-bristle brush to gently agitate the stained area. Rinse: Rinse the fabric thoroughly to remove all soap residue. Dry: Blot the area dry with a clean cloth and always allow the fabric to air dry.

chunks.pageTitle: Cleaning and Washing Your Cubby Bed Canopy
chunks.text: In the event of a significant mess, you can safely machine wash the canopy by following these safety steps: Secure the Zippers: Ensure all zippers are fully closed before washing to prevent snagging or damage to the mesh. Cycle Settings: Use cold water and the delicate cycle only. Detergent: Use normal amounts of a mild laundry detergent. Do not use harsh chemicals or bleach. Drying (Crucial): Always allow the fabric to air dry. Never put the Cubby Bed canopy in a machine dryer, as high heat can damage the fabric's integrity and specialized fit.
